---
title: "Forecasting Monthly Banana Prices"
author: "Jackson Wang"
date: "May 19, 2026"
output: pdf_document
---

# Executive Summary

This report evaluates monthly U.S. banana prices from January 1990 through January 2026 using FRED series `APU0000711211`. The missing October 2025 value is replaced by the assigned estimate of 0.667 dollars per pound.

The purpose is to identify trend, seasonality, transformations, and autocorrelation patterns, then compare several forecasting models including Mean, Naive, Drift, Seasonal Naive, AR(1), ETS, and ARIMA models.

# Business Objective

Banana prices are an important input for short-term food cost planning, and monthly price movements may affect purchasing, budgeting, and inventory decisions.

# Reproducible script

The cleaned R script is available at:

```text
scripts/banana_price_forecasting.R
```

# Model Comparison

The final 12 observations are used as a test set. The Drift model performs best on the test set, with RMSE of 0.0315 and MAPE of 4.2741%.

# Conclusion

The Drift model is selected as the preferred practical forecasting model. It provides the strongest out-of-sample performance and an interpretable baseline for 12-month-ahead banana price planning.
